free_text: 1-F-7/2 neutron hole strength in Ni-59 with the
Ni-60(p,d)Ni-59 reaction at 94 MeV
. The beam polarizations were about
+77% and -74% for the two spin orientations.
A 3.3 mg/cm**2 thick, self-supporting, rolled Ni-60
foil with an enrichment of 99.8%, was used.
Qdd-multiple magnetic spectrometer.
. A position-sensitive helical
proportional counter followed by two plastic
scintillation counters for particle identification.
Shell-model calculations.
DWBA analysis.
Spins and relative form factors.
. last checking has been done.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections in all Subents. BIB update. STATUS moved
to Subents 002-032. SF9=EXP deleted from all
REACTION codes.
Absolute cross sections were determined from the areal
density of the target (determined by weighting) the
spectrometer solid angle, and the integrated charge.
The overall absolute accuracy (see ANALYSIS)
By CAJaD. fig. 2 from PR/C,28,642,1983
SD: DATA-ERR -> ERR-DIG; ERR-T -> DATA-ERR.
ANALYSIS was added. ERR-ANALYS update. DATA -> DATA-CM.
This error bar reflects statistical error and
uncertainty associated with background subtraction.
By CAJaD. fig. 2 from PR/C,28,642,1983
* by authors *
a special problem was encountered regarding the doublet
at  1.30-1.34  MeV,  which  could not be resolved
experimentally.  The peak fitting program was not able
to disentangle reliably the contributions from the two
states, and  hence no individual cross section angular
distributions were obtainable. However, the  analyzing
powers  for the two transitions could be extracted by
taking narrow slices from the left and right sides of
the unresolved  doublet peak, which were identical for
the two spin directions.
Absolute cross sections were determined from the areal
density of the target (determined by weighting) the
spectrometer solid angle, and the integrated charge.
The overall absolute accuracy (see ANALYSIS)
